description: General household rubbish or municipal solid waste sent to landfill, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Disposal or treatment of mixed household rubbish in landfill by mass; recycling and composting are excluded.
source_note: Included because household waste guidance and WARM-style material accounting distinguish landfill from recycling and composting pathways.

description: Mixed packaging waste sent to landfill, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Landfill treatment of mixed packaging material by mass when the user states a disposal context.
source_note: Included because household packaging is a common personal waste stream and disposal method must remain explicit.

description: Plastic waste sent to landfill or general rubbish, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Landfill or general-rubbish disposal of plastic material by mass; recyclable material alone does not imply recycling.
source_note: Included because plastic is a common WARM material class and the V2 ontology separates material from disposal method.

description: Cardboard waste sent to landfill, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Landfill treatment of cardboard by mass; recycling requires an explicit recycling context.
source_note: Included because paper and cardboard are common household material streams in public waste guidance.

description: Paper waste sent to landfill, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Landfill treatment of paper by mass; recycling requires an explicit recycling context.
source_note: Included because paper appears as a common household material stream in waste reduction and materials management references.

description: Food scraps or food waste sent to landfill, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Landfill disposal of food scraps by mass; composting is separate and requires explicit compost context.
source_note: Included because food waste is a common household stream and public guidance distinguishes prevention, composting, and landfill disposal.

description: Plastic material recycled, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Recycling treatment of plastic material by mass; throwing away or general waste is excluded.
source_note: Included because recycling is a distinct treatment route in household waste guidance and WARM material accounting.

description: Cardboard material recycled, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Recycling treatment of cardboard by mass; landfill disposal is excluded.
source_note: Included because cardboard is a common household recyclable material stream.

description: Paper material recycled, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Recycling treatment of paper by mass; landfill disposal is excluded.
source_note: Included because paper is a common recyclable household material stream.

description: Glass material recycled, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Recycling treatment of glass by mass; landfill disposal is excluded.
source_note: Included because glass bottles and containers are common household recyclable materials.

description: Metal cans or packaging recycled, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Recycling treatment of metal containers or packaging by mass; landfill disposal is excluded.
source_note: Included because metal cans are a common recyclable household material stream.

description: Food scraps composted, measured by mass.
calculation_boundary: Composting treatment of food scraps by mass; landfill disposal is separate.
source_note: Included because food recovery guidance distinguishes composting from disposal and prevention.

description: Prepared coffee beverage purchased by serving or item count.
calculation_boundary: One prepared coffee beverage serving; milk variants, packaging, and delivery travel are excluded unless separately modeled.
source_note: Included because drinks are common daily purchases and Ticket 10 requires count-based coffee to stay separate from spend-based factors.

description: Coffee purchase represented by spend, only for money-based factors.
calculation_boundary: Spend-based coffee purchase factor; price is not converted into cups or servings.
source_note: Included to preserve the hard boundary that money inputs may only use money-compatible factors.

description: Beef purchased by mass.
calculation_boundary: Purchased beef mass only; cooking energy, restaurant service, and delivery travel are excluded.
source_note: Included because food research consistently identifies beef as a high-impact common food product and weight is an appropriate activity measure when available.

description: Beef burrito or similar beef-containing takeaway meal by serving.
calculation_boundary: One beef burrito serving; drinks, sides, packaging, and delivery travel are excluded unless separately modeled.
source_note: Included because users commonly describe meals by item count; it remains a maintained serving pathway rather than a top-level food category.

description: Burger purchased as a takeaway or restaurant meal item.
calculation_boundary: One burger serving; fries, drinks, and delivery travel are separate pathways.
source_note: Included because burgers are common meal language and should not be dropped when a maintained serving fallback exists.

description: Fries or hot chips purchased as a meal side by serving.
calculation_boundary: One serving of fries or hot chips; delivery travel is separate.
source_note: Included because sides are common meal components and should remain separate from the main meal item.

description: Pizza purchased as a restaurant or takeaway item by count.
calculation_boundary: One pizza item or serving as described; delivery travel is separate.
source_note: Included because pizza is a common meal item and can use a maintained serving fallback when present.

description: Sandwich purchased as a meal item by count.
calculation_boundary: One sandwich serving; drinks, sides, and delivery travel are separate pathways.
source_note: Included because sandwiches are common personal meal purchases and should not be hidden as generic groceries.

description: Generic restaurant meal by serving when a compatible maintained factor exists.
calculation_boundary: One generic restaurant meal serving; only safe when a compatible maintained factor exists.
source_note: Included as a reviewed generic meal boundary, but broad meal factors must not override a more specific product factor.

description: Takeaway meal by serving when a compatible maintained factor exists.
calculation_boundary: One takeaway meal serving; delivery travel is excluded unless distance and transport mode are separately provided.
source_note: Included because takeaway is common journal language, with delivery transport kept outside the meal factor boundary.

description: Groceries represented by compatible weight-based factor when the product mix is supported.
calculation_boundary: Groceries by mass only when a compatible broad grocery factor is maintained; product mix uncertainty must remain visible.
source_note: Included to document a cautious grocery boundary; broad purchases remain unresolved without compatible factor metadata.

description: Groceries represented by spend, only for compatible money-based factors.
calculation_boundary: Spend-based grocery purchase factor; spend is not converted into product mass or item count.
source_note: Included to preserve the money-only boundary for spend inputs.

description: Soft drink purchased by serving or item count.
calculation_boundary: One soft drink serving; packaging and delivery travel are separate pathways.
source_note: Included because drinks often appear alongside meals and should not make the whole meal partial.

description: Milk bought as a retail grocery item.
calculation_boundary: One retail milk item; exact container size is not inferred unless provided by a compatible factor.
source_note: Included because milk is common grocery language, with quantity uncertainty made visible through fallback confidence.

description: Bread bought as a retail grocery item or loaf.
calculation_boundary: One bread item or loaf; exact weight is not inferred unless provided by a compatible factor.
source_note: Included because bread is a common grocery item and can be represented through a maintained item fallback.

description: Snack or snack packet bought as a grocery item.
calculation_boundary: One snack item or serving; exact product mix remains approximate.
source_note: Included because snacks are common grocery language but need a visibly approximate fallback boundary.

description: Clothing purchase by item count when a compatible maintained factor exists.
calculation_boundary: Clothing item purchase; material, brand, and lifecycle details are not inferred.
source_note: Included because clothing is a common goods purchase, but estimation requires a compatible item factor or remains unresolved.

description: Clothing purchase by spend when a compatible money-based factor exists.
calculation_boundary: Spend-based clothing purchase factor; spend is not converted into item count.
source_note: Included to document a cautious spend pathway for clothing purchases.

description: Electronics purchase by item count when a compatible maintained factor exists.
calculation_boundary: Electronics purchase by item; electricity use is a separate energy pathway.
source_note: Included because electronics are common goods purchases but require compatible product factor metadata before estimation is safe.

description: Electronics purchase by spend when a compatible money-based factor exists.
calculation_boundary: Spend-based electronics purchase factor; spend is not converted into item count or use-phase energy.
source_note: Included to document a cautious spend pathway for electronics purchases.

description: Generic passenger car travel by vehicle distance when fuel is unknown or generic.
calculation_boundary: Passenger car distance; fuel-specific factors should be preferred when explicit fuel evidence exists.
source_note: Included because passenger cars are a major household transport activity and distance is the maintained calculation quantity.

description: Petrol passenger car travel by vehicle distance.
calculation_boundary: Petrol passenger car travel by distance; occupancy and upstream fuel details are not inferred.
source_note: Included because fuel-specific car factors are common and explicit fuel evidence should rank above generic car metadata.

description: Diesel passenger car travel by vehicle distance.
calculation_boundary: Diesel passenger car travel by distance; occupancy and upstream fuel details are not inferred.
source_note: Included because explicit diesel evidence should rank diesel factors above generic car factors.

description: Battery-electric passenger car travel by vehicle distance.
calculation_boundary: Electric passenger car travel by distance; charging electricity source is not inferred unless represented by the factor.
source_note: Included because explicit electric vehicle evidence must not be collapsed into generic petrol defaults.

description: Hybrid passenger car travel by vehicle distance.
calculation_boundary: Hybrid passenger car travel by distance when compatible factor metadata exists.
source_note: Included because hybrid is a common explicit user trait and should be treated as a controlled fuel type.

description: Bus or coach ride by passenger distance.
calculation_boundary: Passenger bus distance; no distance is inferred when absent.
source_note: Included because public transport by passenger distance is a common personal transport pathway.

description: Train, rail, tram, subway, or metro ride by passenger distance.
calculation_boundary: Passenger rail distance; route-specific rail traits are used only when explicitly supplied or maintained.
source_note: Included because rail and metro modes are common personal transport pathways measured by passenger distance.

description: Taxi, cab, or rideshare trip by distance.
calculation_boundary: Taxi or rideshare passenger distance; no detour, occupancy, or vehicle traits are inferred unless supplied.
source_note: Included because rideshare and taxi are common journal terms that should not select generic private-car factors when taxi factors exist.

description: Passenger flight by passenger distance when compatible flight factor metadata exists.
calculation_boundary: Passenger flight distance; class, route type, and RF effect use maintained defaults only when visible.
source_note: Included because flights are a common high-impact personal transport pathway but require safe factor defaults and explicit distance.

description: Walking or running distance with no powered operational-emissions estimate.
calculation_boundary: No powered operational emissions are estimated for personal walking or running.
source_note: Included to make the non-estimated operational boundary explicit rather than returning a misleading transport factor.

description: Cycling distance with no powered operational-emissions estimate.
calculation_boundary: No powered operational emissions are estimated for personal cycling.
source_note: Included to make the non-estimated operational boundary explicit rather than returning a misleading transport factor.

description: Household electricity consumption measured in kWh.
calculation_boundary: Electricity supplied to household devices by kWh; end-use devices are descriptive unless separately modeled.
source_note: Included because residential electricity is a common household emissions pathway and kWh is the maintained activity quantity.

description: Electric space heater electricity consumption measured directly or derived from power and duration.
calculation_boundary: Electric heater electricity use by kWh; default power assumptions must remain visible when wattage is missing.
source_note: Included because heating duration is common journal language and must resolve to an electricity factor only after kWh is known or derived.

description: Air conditioning or cooling electricity consumption measured in kWh.
calculation_boundary: Cooling electricity use by kWh; vague duration is not converted into kWh without a maintained assumption.
source_note: Included as a common household electricity end use, but it remains unresolved unless energy can be built safely.

description: Hot water energy use represented by compatible kWh-based factor when supported.
calculation_boundary: Hot water electricity use by kWh only; shower duration is not converted without maintained device assumptions.
source_note: Included because water heating is a common household energy use, with a strict no-fabricated-kWh boundary.

description: Electric cooking appliance electricity consumption measured in kWh.
calculation_boundary: Electric cooking energy by kWh; duration alone is not converted unless maintained appliance power assumptions are available.
source_note: Included as a common household energy end use with strict quantity requirements.

description: Generic device electricity consumption measured directly in kWh.
calculation_boundary: Generic device electricity use by kWh; vague duration or unspecified device power remains unresolved.
source_note: Included because device use is common, but kWh or maintained power assumptions are required before estimation.

description: PC or laptop electricity consumption measured in kWh when provided directly.
calculation_boundary: Computer electricity use by kWh; gaming duration alone remains unresolved unless maintained power assumptions exist.
source_note: Included to separate use-phase electricity from electronics purchase pathways.

description: TV, console, or entertainment device electricity consumption measured in kWh when provided directly.
calculation_boundary: Entertainment device electricity use by kWh; duration alone remains unresolved unless maintained power assumptions exist.
source_note: Included to separate use-phase electricity from electronics purchase pathways.
